name: Caturra

migrationPath.notes: Natural dwarf mutation of Bourbon discovered
Introduced and widely promoted for compact size and yield

description: A natural dwarf mutation of Bourbon discovered in Minas Gerais, Brazil around 1915. Its compact size allows for denser planting and easier harvesting, making it a workhorse of Latin American coffee production. It became a parent variety for many important hybrids including Catuai and the Catimor group. Despite its widespread planting, Caturra is highly susceptible to all major coffee diseases.
